purpose: Provides a probabilistic portfolio construction framework under correlation regime uncertainty by explicitly modeling the unconditional correlation as a mixture of two distinct regimes rather than a point estimate.
components: Regime D (demand-dominant): correlation -0.30 to -0.50, prior probability 55-60%
Regime S (supply-dominant): correlation +0.20 to +0.40, prior probability 40-45%
Unconditional correlation: mixture average ~-0.06 (robust to ±0.15 perturbation in weights)
Key insight: using the near-zero unconditional correlation for mean-variance optimization is suboptimal because the bimodal distribution violates normality assumptions
Application: portfolio construction should hedge against both modes rather than optimizing for the mean
current_assessment: More actionable for portfolio construction than conjunction trigger approaches per pair_2 because it provides explicit probability weights and conditional return distributions. The specific regime probabilities are calibrated judgment, not model output, but the near-zero unconditional is robust to plausible perturbations.
limitations: Regime probabilities are subjective expert calibrations, not derived from a statistical model
Does not account for regime transition dynamics or persistence
Assumes only two regimes — reality may involve additional states or a continuum
Implementation requires either regime identification (hard) or hedging against both modes (costly)
The 25-30% recession base rate (challenger_01) may make the 55-60% Regime D weight understated
